articles.headline: India Unveils Comprehensive Green Hydrogen Policy to Boost Energy Transition, Achieve Net Zero Goals
articles.explainer:
• The policy aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• It outlines significant incentives for production, demand creation across key sectors, and robust R&D.
• Crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors such as refining, fertilizers, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
• Expected to create significant employment opportunities, attract substantial domestic and foreign investment, and reduce import dependency.
• This initiative is a cornerstone of India's commitment to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070 and enhance energy security.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure: Energy
Investment Models
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Conservation
Science & Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target for green hydrogen production capacity.
Key components of the policy: incentives, R&D, demand creation, manufacturing.
Applications of green hydrogen in various industrial sectors.
Year of India's Net Zero target (2070).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the economic benefits (job creation, export potential, investment) and challenges (cost, infrastructure, technology) of the policy.
Evaluate the environmental impact and contribution of green hydrogen to India's climate goals.
Examine the role of government incentives and private sector participation in achieving the policy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and electrolysers.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Independence of Election Commission, Issues Guidelines for Appointments
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the limits of executive discretion in the appointment of Election Commissioners.
• The ruling aims to reinforce the autonomy and neutrality of the Election Commission of India (ECI), a cornerstone of democratic governance.
• It establishes a framework for a neutral committee to oversee the selection process, reducing potential executive overreach.
• The decision is seen as a significant step towards ensuring free and fair elections and strengthening constitutional bodies.
• This judgment follows long-standing debates and petitions regarding the transparency and impartiality of the appointment process for ECI members.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article related to Election Commission (Article 324).
Powers and functions of the Election Commission.
Composition of the Election Commission.
Key aspects of the Supreme Court's guidelines for appointments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of an independent Election Commission for the health of Indian democracy.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the balance of power between the executive and constitutional bodies.
Evaluate the arguments for and against a collegium-like system for appointing Election Commissioners.
Suggest further reforms that could enhance the autonomy and efficiency of the ECI.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen, appointed in 1950.

articles.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint River Commission Finalizes Framework for Teesta Water Sharing Agreement
articles.explainer:
• After years of protracted negotiations, the Joint River Commission (JRC) between India and Bangladesh has reached a consensus on a framework for sharing Teesta river waters.
• The agreement addresses long-standing concerns of both nations regarding equitable water distribution, particularly during the lean season.
• This breakthrough is expected to significantly improve bilateral relations and foster greater regional cooperation in water management.
• The framework includes provisions for data sharing, joint technical studies, and a dispute resolution mechanism.
• The Teesta river is a critical lifeline for agriculture and livelihoods in both West Bengal (India) and Bangladesh.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Geographical location and significance of the Teesta River.
Key aspects of the newly agreed framework (data sharing, dispute resolution).
Other major transboundary rivers shared by India and Bangladesh.
Role of the Joint River Commission.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and challenges in India-Bangladesh water sharing agreements, particularly concerning the Teesta.
Discuss the implications of this agreement for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and regional stability.
Evaluate the potential economic, social, and environmental benefits of equitable water sharing for both nations.
Suggest ways to build on this success for broader cooperation in transboundary river management in South Asia.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Teesta River flowing through India and Bangladesh.
articles.trivia: The Teesta River originates in the Pahunri glacier in Sikkim, India.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Expresses Cautious Optimism on Economic Outlook Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor, in a post-Monetary Policy Review statement, indicated cautious optimism regarding India's economic trajectory.
• While acknowledging global economic uncertainties and inflationary pressures, the Governor highlighted India's robust domestic demand and resilient financial sector.
• Key focus areas include maintaining price stability, supporting growth, and ensuring financial sector resilience against external shocks.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to data-driven policy decisions, with a flexible approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts suggest that while inflation remains a concern, the central bank sees room for sustained growth driven by government capital expenditure and private consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation and its impact
prelimsPoints: Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy.
Instruments of monetary policy (Repo rate, CRR, etc.).
Current inflation target range set by the government for RBI.
Meaning of 'cautious optimism' in economic context.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the impact of global economic headwinds (e.g., geopolitical conflicts, supply chain disruptions) on India's economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in achieving its objectives.
Suggest fiscal and structural reforms that could complement monetary policy efforts to ensure sustainable economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: RBI headquarters building in Mumbai with the Indian Rupee symbol.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-X' Enhancing Disaster Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone with the successful launch of 'EOS-X', an advanced Earth Observation Satellite.
• Launched aboard the PSLV-CXX rocket from Sriharikota, EOS-X is designed to provide high-resolution imagery and data for disaster management, agriculture, and urban planning.
• The satellite features enhanced spectral bands and improved revisit capabilities, offering more timely and accurate information.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for societal benefit.
• Data from EOS-X will be crucial for predicting natural disasters, monitoring crop health, and assessing environmental changes.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Full form of ISRO and its primary objectives.
Differences between PSLV and GSLV.
Applications of Earth Observation Satellites.
Location of India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology, particularly Earth Observation Satellites, in disaster management and sustainable development in India.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous capabilities contribute to India's strategic autonomy and global standing in space.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits derived from satellite data in sectors like agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space program, including private sector participation and international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: ISRO's PSLV rocket launching into space from Sriharikota.
articles.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites) in 2017.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Nari' Program to Boost Women's Economic Empowerment and Entrepreneurship
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Saksham Nari' program, a comprehensive initiative aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment.
• The program focuses on providing skill development, financial literacy, access to credit, and market linkages for women entrepreneurs.
• It integrates various existing schemes and introduces new components to create a holistic support ecosystem for women.
• 'Saksham Nari' seeks to address gender disparities in the workforce and promote women's leadership in economic activities.
• Special emphasis is placed on rural women and those from marginalized communities, ensuring inclusive growth and development.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Role of women and women’s organization
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for 'Saksham Nari' program.
Key pillars of the program (skill development, financial access, market linkages).
Examples of other government schemes for women's empowerment.
Target beneficiaries of the program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by women in achieving economic empowerment in India.
Analyze how the 'Saksham Nari' program aims to address these challenges and its potential impact on gender equality.
Evaluate the role of skill development, financial literacy, and access to credit in fostering women's entrepreneurship.
Suggest measures to ensure the effective implementation and long-term sustainability of such welfare programs for women.
articles.imageDescription: Group of women participating in a skill development workshop.
articles.trivia: India has seen a significant rise in women-led startups in recent years, contributing to economic growth and innovation.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Critical Threat to Western Ghats Biodiversity, Urges Enhanced Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) has flagged an alarming rate of biodiversity loss in the Western Ghats.
• The report points to increasing habitat fragmentation, climate change impacts, and unsustainable developmental activities as primary drivers of this decline.
• It calls for immediate and enhanced conservation measures, including stricter enforcement of environmental regulations and community-based conservation models.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• The study emphasizes the critical role of the Western Ghats in India's ecological balance, including its influence on monsoon patterns and water security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Climate change
prelimsPoints: Location and geographical extent of the Western Ghats.
Status of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Major threats identified in the report.
Examples of endemic species found in the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the recommendations of the NCBS report and suggest a multi-stakeholder approach for effective conservation.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing development needs with environmental protection in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
Examine the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in biodiversity conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest and a waterfall.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: India Unveils National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 to Bolster Digital Defenses
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has launched India's updated National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure, promoting cyber resilience, and fostering a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It emphasizes a multi-pronged approach involving government, industry, academia, and international cooperation to combat evolving cyber threats.
• Key components include enhancing threat intelligence sharing, establishing specialized cyber incident response teams, and developing indigenous cybersecurity solutions.
• This updated strategy is crucial for protecting India's digital economy, national security, and citizens' data in an increasingly interconnected world.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Cyber Security
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Nodal agency for cybersecurity in India (CERT-In).
Key pillars of the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0.
Importance of Critical Information Infrastructure.
The Information Technology Act, 2000.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key features of the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 and its potential effectiveness in building cyber resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cybersecurity framework, including technological, human, and legal aspects.
Suggest measures for enhancing international cooperation in combating transboundary cybercrime and cyber warfare.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a map of India, representing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India is among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally, making robust cybersecurity strategies essential.

articles.headline: Veteran Jammu & Kashmir Leader Dina Nath Bhagat Passes Away at 79, Remembered for Public Service
articles.explainer:
• Dina Nath Bhagat, a veteran politician from Jammu and Kashmir, passed away at the age of 79.
• He served as an MLA in the Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly since 2014, representing his constituency with dedication.
• Bhagat was known for his significant contributions to regional development and his advocacy for the welfare of marginalized communities.
• His political career spanned several decades, during which he witnessed and actively participated in critical junctures of J&K's political history.
• Leaders across the political spectrum mourned his demise, acknowledging his commitment to public service and democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the role of regional political leaders in shaping state-level policies and advocating for local needs.
Analyze the evolving political landscape of Jammu and Kashmir since the abrogation of Article 370 and the role of veteran leaders.
Examine the challenges faced by politicians in conflict-affected regions and their contributions to peace and development.
Reflect on the importance of democratic representation and public service in strengthening state legislatures.
articles.trivia: Jammu and Kashmir transitioned from a state to a Union Territory in 2019, with a legislative assembly.

articles.headline: Former Odisha MLA Rajendra Dholakia Passes Away at 68, Remembered for Legislative Contributions
articles.explainer:
• Rajendra Dholakia, a seasoned politician and former Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Odisha, passed away at 68.
• He had a distinguished career, serving as an MLA from 2004–2014 and again since 2019, contributing significantly to state legislative affairs.
• Dholakia was known for his articulate interventions in the assembly and his focus on issues pertinent to his constituency and the state.
• His demise marks the loss of a prominent voice in Odisha's political landscape.
• Political leaders and constituents alike paid tribute to his commitment to democratic principles and public welfare.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the role and responsibilities of a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) in India's federal structure.
Analyze the importance of experienced politicians in maintaining legislative continuity and institutional knowledge.
Examine how state legislatures function as platforms for public discourse and policy-making.
Reflect on the significance of local representation in addressing regional disparities and promoting balanced development.
articles.trivia: The Odisha Legislative Assembly has 147 elected members.

articles.headline: Acclaimed Journalist Sankarshan Thakur Passes Away at 63, Champion of Investigative Reporting
articles.explainer:
• Sankarshan Thakur, a highly respected journalist and editor of 'The Telegraph' since 2023, passed away at the age of 63.
• Known for his incisive political commentary, investigative reporting, and eloquent writing style, Thakur had a profound impact on Indian journalism.
• His career spanned several decades, during which he covered major political events and contributed to various leading publications.
• Thakur was a strong advocate for press freedom, journalistic ethics, and unbiased reporting, earning him accolades and respect across the media fraternity.
• His demise is a significant loss to Indian journalism, leaving behind a legacy of integrity and intellectual rigor.
gsSubTopics: Role of media and social networking sites in Indian democracy
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article related to freedom of press in India.
Role of media in a democracy.
Major challenges faced by Indian journalism today.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of traditional media in the age of digital journalism and social media.
Analyze the challenges to press freedom and journalistic ethics in contemporary India.
Evaluate the importance of investigative reporting in holding power accountable and informing public discourse.
Suggest measures to strengthen the credibility and independence of the media in a democratic society.
articles.trivia: World Press Freedom Day is celebrated annually on May 3 to raise awareness of the importance of freedom of the press.

articles.headline: Veteran Actress Priya Marathe Passes Away at 38, Remembered for Contributions to Marathi Television
articles.explainer:
• Priya Marathe, a popular actress known for her significant work in Marathi and Hindi television, passed away at the age of 38.
• She gained widespread recognition for her roles in hit shows like 'Pavitra Rishta,' 'Ya Sukhano Ya,' and 'Tuzech Mi Geet Gaat Aahe'.
• Marathe was celebrated for her versatile acting skills and her ability to portray diverse characters, leaving a lasting impact on her audience.
• Her untimely demise has saddened the Indian entertainment industry, with tributes pouring in from colleagues and fans.
• Her work contributed significantly to the growth and popularity of regional television, showcasing its rich cultural narratives.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the growing prominence of regional cinema and television in India's cultural landscape.
Analyze the role of entertainment as a soft power tool and its contribution to national integration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for artists in the Indian television and film industry.
Reflect on how popular culture can reflect and influence societal values and norms.
articles.trivia: Marathi cinema is the oldest film industry in India, with the first full-length Indian feature film, 'Raja Harishchandra,' being a Marathi film by Dadasaheb Phalke in 1913.

articles.headline: Monsoon Progress Fuels Optimism for Kharif Crops, Government Assures MSP Support to Farmers
articles.explainer:
• Favorable monsoon rainfall across key agricultural regions has generated optimism for a robust Kharif crop yield this year.
• The Ministry of Agriculture reported significant progress in sowing operations, particularly for rice, pulses, and oilseeds.
• The government reiterated its commitment to ensuring Minimum Support Price (MSP) for major crops, aiming to safeguard farmers' income.
• Timely monsoon has alleviated concerns over potential drought conditions in some areas earlier in the season.
• This positive agricultural outlook is crucial for rural economy stability, food security, and controlling food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: Major crops grown during Kharif season.
Purpose of Minimum Support Price (MSP).
Factors influencing monsoon rainfall in India.
Role of the Food Corporation of India (FCI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the monsoon in India's agricultural economy and its impact on food security.
Analyze the effectiveness of Minimum Support Price (MSP) in ensuring farmers' income and its implications for agricultural markets.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian agriculture, including climate change, market access, and infrastructure deficits.
Suggest policy measures to enhance agricultural productivity, farmer welfare, and resilience against climatic shocks.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a lush green paddy field during monsoon season.
articles.trivia: Kharif crops are also known as 'monsoon crops' and are typically sown with the onset of the first rains in July.

articles.headline: India and France Conclude Joint Naval Exercise 'Varuna-XXIII' in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and the French Navy successfully concluded the 23rd edition of their bilateral naval exercise, 'Varuna-XXIII', in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The exercise involved a wide range of naval operations, including anti-submarine warfare, air defense drills, and surface combat maneuvers.
• 'Varuna' aims to enhance interoperability, share best practices, and strengthen maritime cooperation between the two navies.
• This joint exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in ensuring security and stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• The collaboration is vital for addressing common maritime challenges, including piracy, counter-terrorism, and safeguarding sea lanes of communication.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Name of the joint naval exercise between India and France.
Location of the exercise (Indian Ocean Region).
Other major bilateral military exercises India conducts with key partners.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze how joint naval exercises like 'Varuna' contribute to strengthening India's defense capabilities and fostering international cooperation.
Evaluate the evolving strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in the context of the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the common maritime security challenges faced by nations in the IOR and the role of multilateral cooperation in addressing them.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships conducting joint maneuvers in the ocean.
articles.trivia: India and France also conduct joint army exercise 'Shakti' and air force exercise 'Garuda'.

articles.headline: Government Overhauls Public Grievance Redressal System for Enhanced Efficiency and Transparency
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) announced a major overhaul of the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS).
• The updated system incorporates AI-driven analytics, faster response mechanisms, and a citizen-centric feedback loop.
• The aim is to improve the speed and quality of grievance redressal, making government services more accessible and accountable.
• Training programs for government officials on effective grievance handling and citizen communication are also being rolled out.
• This initiative reflects the government's commitment to 'Good Governance' and leveraging technology for improved public service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy.
articles.source: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG)
prelimsPoints: Full form of CPGRAMS and DARPG.
Key features of the overhauled system (AI, faster response).
Concept of 'Good Governance'.
Examples of other e-governance initiatives in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of an effective public grievance redressal mechanism in a democratic and welfare state.
Analyze how the integration of technology and AI can enhance the efficiency and transparency of public service delivery.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing administrative reforms and ensuring their sustained impact on citizen satisfaction.
Suggest measures to foster a culture of accountability and responsiveness within the bureaucracy.
articles.imageDescription: A hand pressing a 'feedback' button on a digital interface.
articles.trivia: CPGRAMS was launched in 2007 as an online system for citizens to lodge grievances with Central Government organizations.

articles.headline: India Proposes Global Roadmap for Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Digital Economy Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India, hosting the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, proposed a global roadmap for leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for inclusive growth.
• The proposal emphasizes interoperable digital solutions like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as models for developing nations.
• Discussions focused on addressing the digital divide, promoting cross-border data flows with privacy safeguards, and fostering digital skill development.
• India highlighted its success in building DPI at scale, demonstrating its potential for financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
• The initiative aims to build a common framework for digital cooperation among G20 nations and extend benefits globally.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Key examples of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (Aadhaar, UPI).
Objectives of G20 Digital Economy Working Group.
Concept of 'digital divide'.
Significance of India's G20 leadership in digital transformation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its potential to drive inclusive growth and development, citing Indian examples.
Analyze India's role and proposals at the G20 Digital Economy Meeting and their implications for global digital cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in promoting cross-border data flows while ensuring data privacy and security.
Suggest how developing nations can leverage DPI models to accelerate their digital transformation and achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with digital network lines in the background.
articles.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems, processing billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Conducts High-Level Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, vocational education integration, teacher training, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, resource allocation, and achieving equitable access to quality education were discussed.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century, promoting holistic and multidisciplinary learning.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to accelerating implementation, with a focus on foundational literacy and numeracy, and early childhood care and education.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Year of launch of National Education Policy.
Key pillars of NEP 2020 (e.g., access, equity, quality, accountability).
Concept of 'foundational literacy and numeracy'.
Role of vocational education in NEP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Analyze the key reforms proposed under NEP 2020 and their expected impact on students, teachers, and institutions.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, funding, and teacher readiness.
Suggest strategies for ensuring equitable access to quality education and promoting holistic development across all levels of schooling.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those of 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant, India's Indigenous Aircraft Carrier, Completes Maiden Operational Deployment
articles.explainer:
• INS Vikrant, India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier, successfully concluded its maiden operational deployment in the Arabian Sea.
• The deployment involved extensive flight operations, integration of various fighter aircraft, and complex tactical maneuvers with other naval assets.
• This milestone significantly enhances India's maritime power projection capabilities and reinforces its position as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The successful deployment showcases India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing and advanced naval technology.
• INS Vikrant's operational readiness is crucial for safeguarding India's vast coastline, island territories, and strategic maritime interests.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Name of India's first indigenous aircraft carrier.
Significance of an aircraft carrier in naval power.
Operational area of the deployment (Arabian Sea).
Concept of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of aircraft carriers in modern naval warfare and India's maritime doctrine.
Analyze the significance of INS Vikrant's indigenous design and construction for India's defense self-reliance and technological capabilities.
Evaluate how the deployment of INS Vikrant enhances India's power projection and security role in the Indian Ocean Region.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's naval modernization program, including the acquisition of more indigenous platforms.
articles.imageDescription: INS Vikrant sailing in the open sea with fighter jets on its deck.
articles.trivia: INS Vikrant is named after India's first aircraft carrier, which was acquired from the United Kingdom in 1961.

highlights: India launches ambitious Green Hydrogen Policy to become a global hub, vital for Net Zero goals and energy security.
Supreme Court reinforces Election Commission's independence by issuing new guidelines for appointment of Election Commissioners.
India and Bangladesh finalize framework for long-pending Teesta water sharing agreement, boosting bilateral ties.
ISRO's EOS-X satellite launch enhances Earth observation capabilities for disaster management and agriculture.
Government introduces 'Saksham Nari' program to empower women economically through skill development and entrepreneurship.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's recently unveiled Green Hydrogen Policy, consider the following statements:
1. The policy aims to make India a net exporter of green hydrogen.
2. It primarily focuses on decarbonizing the power generation sector.
3. Financial incentives like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme are part of the policy.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The policy aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is incorrect: While power generation can benefit, the policy primarily focuses on hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel. Statement 3 is correct: The policy outlines incentives for production, demand creation, and R&D, often involving schemes like PLI.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the Election Commission of India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Election Commission for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Varuna' exercise is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and which of the following countries?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Varuna' exercise is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between the Indian Navy and the French Navy. It enhances interoperability and maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Western Ghats is/are correct?
1. It is older than the Himalayan mountains.
2. It is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
3. It significantly influences India's monsoon patterns.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Western Ghats are indeed older than the Himalayas, are a recognized biodiversity hotspot, and play a crucial role in India's monsoon climate.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and their primary function in India:
1. Aadhaar: Unique identity authentication
2. UPI: Real-time digital payments
3. DigiLocker: Digital document storage and verification
Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. Aadhaar provides unique identity, UPI enables real-time payments, and DigiLocker facilitates digital storage and verification of documents.

mainsMocks.question: India's new Green Hydrogen Policy is a strategic move towards achieving Net Zero emissions and enhancing energy security. Discuss its potential benefits and the key challenges in its implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Environmental Pollution & Degradation, Conservation; Infrastructure: Energy; Science & Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the policy and its dual objectives (Net Zero, energy security). Discuss benefits like decarbonization of industries, job creation, export potential, and reduced import bill. Analyze challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, technological advancements, and policy support mechanisms. Conclude with the importance of a multi-stakeholder approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent judgment on the appointment of Election Commissioners aims to reinforce the independence of the Election Commission of India. Critically analyze the significance of this ruling for India's democratic framework and suggest further measures to strengthen constitutional bodies.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the context of the Supreme Court's ruling and the importance of ECI's independence. Discuss how the ruling (e.g., establishing a neutral committee) enhances transparency and reduces executive influence. Analyze its impact on free and fair elections and the principle of separation of powers. Suggest measures like constitutional amendments for fixed tenure, financial autonomy, and robust impeachment processes for all constitutional bodies.

mainsMocks.question: Transboundary river water disputes pose significant challenges to bilateral relations in South Asia. In light of the India-Bangladesh agreement on the Teesta water sharing framework, discuss the role of water diplomacy in fostering regional cooperation and stability.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by highlighting the prevalence of transboundary water disputes in South Asia and the historical context of the Teesta issue. Discuss the significance of the recent India-Bangladesh framework as a success of water diplomacy. Analyze how water diplomacy can build trust, enhance mutual benefits, and promote regional stability. Conclude with recommendations for a comprehensive approach to managing shared water resources in the region.

quickBytes.text: India's Net Zero emission target is set for the year 2070.
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Election Commission of India.
The Teesta River originates in the Pahunri glacier in Sikkim, India.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935.
PSLV is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle for Earth Observation Satellites.
The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a biodiversity hotspot.
CERT-In is the national nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents in India.
Kharif crops are also known as 'monsoon crops' and are typically sown in July.
The India-France joint naval exercise is named 'Varuna'.
CPGRAMS is the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System in India.

GS1.headline: Acclaimed Journalist Sankarshan Thakur Passes Away at 63, Champion of Investigative Reporting
GS1.explainer:
• Sankarshan Thakur, a highly respected journalist and editor of 'The Telegraph' since 2023, passed away at the age of 63.
• Known for his incisive political commentary, investigative reporting, and eloquent writing style, Thakur had a profound impact on Indian journalism.
• His career spanned several decades, during which he covered major political events and contributed to various leading publications.
• Thakur was a strong advocate for press freedom, journalistic ethics, and unbiased reporting, earning him accolades and respect across the media fraternity.
• His demise is a significant loss to Indian journalism, leaving behind a legacy of integrity and intellectual rigor.
gsSubTopics: Role of media and social networking sites in Indian democracy
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article related to freedom of press in India.
Role of media in a democracy.
Major challenges faced by Indian journalism today.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of traditional media in the age of digital journalism and social media.
Analyze the challenges to press freedom and journalistic ethics in contemporary India.
Evaluate the importance of investigative reporting in holding power accountable and informing public discourse.
Suggest measures to strengthen the credibility and independence of the media in a democratic society.
GS1.trivia: World Press Freedom Day is celebrated annually on May 3 to raise awareness of the importance of freedom of the press.

GS1.headline: Veteran Actress Priya Marathe Passes Away at 38, Remembered for Contributions to Marathi Television
GS1.explainer:
• Priya Marathe, a popular actress known for her significant work in Marathi and Hindi television, passed away at the age of 38.
• She gained widespread recognition for her roles in hit shows like 'Pavitra Rishta,' 'Ya Sukhano Ya,' and 'Tuzech Mi Geet Gaat Aahe'.
• Marathe was celebrated for her versatile acting skills and her ability to portray diverse characters, leaving a lasting impact on her audience.
• Her untimely demise has saddened the Indian entertainment industry, with tributes pouring in from colleagues and fans.
• Her work contributed significantly to the growth and popularity of regional television, showcasing its rich cultural narratives.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the growing prominence of regional cinema and television in India's cultural landscape.
Analyze the role of entertainment as a soft power tool and its contribution to national integration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for artists in the Indian television and film industry.
Reflect on how popular culture can reflect and influence societal values and norms.
GS1.trivia: Marathi cinema is the oldest film industry in India, with the first full-length Indian feature film, 'Raja Harishchandra,' being a Marathi film by Dadasaheb Phalke in 1913.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Independence of Election Commission, Issues Guidelines for Appointments
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the limits of executive discretion in the appointment of Election Commissioners.
• The ruling aims to reinforce the autonomy and neutrality of the Election Commission of India (ECI), a cornerstone of democratic governance.
• It establishes a framework for a neutral committee to oversee the selection process, reducing potential executive overreach.
• The decision is seen as a significant step towards ensuring free and fair elections and strengthening constitutional bodies.
• This judgment follows long-standing debates and petitions regarding the transparency and impartiality of the appointment process for ECI members.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article related to Election Commission (Article 324).
Powers and functions of the Election Commission.
Composition of the Election Commission.
Key aspects of the Supreme Court's guidelines for appointments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of an independent Election Commission for the health of Indian democracy.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the balance of power between the executive and constitutional bodies.
Evaluate the arguments for and against a collegium-like system for appointing Election Commissioners.
Suggest further reforms that could enhance the autonomy and efficiency of the ECI.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen, appointed in 1950.

GS2.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint River Commission Finalizes Framework for Teesta Water Sharing Agreement
GS2.explainer:
• After years of protracted negotiations, the Joint River Commission (JRC) between India and Bangladesh has reached a consensus on a framework for sharing Teesta river waters.
• The agreement addresses long-standing concerns of both nations regarding equitable water distribution, particularly during the lean season.
• This breakthrough is expected to significantly improve bilateral relations and foster greater regional cooperation in water management.
• The framework includes provisions for data sharing, joint technical studies, and a dispute resolution mechanism.
• The Teesta river is a critical lifeline for agriculture and livelihoods in both West Bengal (India) and Bangladesh.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Geographical location and significance of the Teesta River.
Key aspects of the newly agreed framework (data sharing, dispute resolution).
Other major transboundary rivers shared by India and Bangladesh.
Role of the Joint River Commission.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and challenges in India-Bangladesh water sharing agreements, particularly concerning the Teesta.
Discuss the implications of this agreement for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and regional stability.
Evaluate the potential economic, social, and environmental benefits of equitable water sharing for both nations.
Suggest ways to build on this success for broader cooperation in transboundary river management in South Asia.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Teesta River flowing through India and Bangladesh.
GS2.trivia: The Teesta River originates in the Pahunri glacier in Sikkim, India.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Nari' Program to Boost Women's Economic Empowerment and Entrepreneurship
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Saksham Nari' program, a comprehensive initiative aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment.
• The program focuses on providing skill development, financial literacy, access to credit, and market linkages for women entrepreneurs.
• It integrates various existing schemes and introduces new components to create a holistic support ecosystem for women.
• 'Saksham Nari' seeks to address gender disparities in the workforce and promote women's leadership in economic activities.
• Special emphasis is placed on rural women and those from marginalized communities, ensuring inclusive growth and development.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Role of women and women’s organization
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for 'Saksham Nari' program.
Key pillars of the program (skill development, financial access, market linkages).
Examples of other government schemes for women's empowerment.
Target beneficiaries of the program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by women in achieving economic empowerment in India.
Analyze how the 'Saksham Nari' program aims to address these challenges and its potential impact on gender equality.
Evaluate the role of skill development, financial literacy, and access to credit in fostering women's entrepreneurship.
Suggest measures to ensure the effective implementation and long-term sustainability of such welfare programs for women.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of women participating in a skill development workshop.
GS2.trivia: India has seen a significant rise in women-led startups in recent years, contributing to economic growth and innovation.

GS2.headline: Veteran Jammu & Kashmir Leader Dina Nath Bhagat Passes Away at 79, Remembered for Public Service
GS2.explainer:
• Dina Nath Bhagat, a veteran politician from Jammu and Kashmir, passed away at the age of 79.
• He served as an MLA in the Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly since 2014, representing his constituency with dedication.
• Bhagat was known for his significant contributions to regional development and his advocacy for the welfare of marginalized communities.
• His political career spanned several decades, during which he witnessed and actively participated in critical junctures of J&K's political history.
• Leaders across the political spectrum mourned his demise, acknowledging his commitment to public service and democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the role of regional political leaders in shaping state-level policies and advocating for local needs.
Analyze the evolving political landscape of Jammu and Kashmir since the abrogation of Article 370 and the role of veteran leaders.
Examine the challenges faced by politicians in conflict-affected regions and their contributions to peace and development.
Reflect on the importance of democratic representation and public service in strengthening state legislatures.
GS2.trivia: Jammu and Kashmir transitioned from a state to a Union Territory in 2019, with a legislative assembly.

GS2.headline: Former Odisha MLA Rajendra Dholakia Passes Away at 68, Remembered for Legislative Contributions
GS2.explainer:
• Rajendra Dholakia, a seasoned politician and former Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Odisha, passed away at 68.
• He had a distinguished career, serving as an MLA from 2004–2014 and again since 2019, contributing significantly to state legislative affairs.
• Dholakia was known for his articulate interventions in the assembly and his focus on issues pertinent to his constituency and the state.
• His demise marks the loss of a prominent voice in Odisha's political landscape.
• Political leaders and constituents alike paid tribute to his commitment to democratic principles and public welfare.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
mainsPoints: Briefly discuss the role and responsibilities of a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) in India's federal structure.
Analyze the importance of experienced politicians in maintaining legislative continuity and institutional knowledge.
Examine how state legislatures function as platforms for public discourse and policy-making.
Reflect on the significance of local representation in addressing regional disparities and promoting balanced development.
GS2.trivia: The Odisha Legislative Assembly has 147 elected members.

GS2.headline: India and France Conclude Joint Naval Exercise 'Varuna-XXIII' in Indian Ocean Region
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and the French Navy successfully concluded the 23rd edition of their bilateral naval exercise, 'Varuna-XXIII', in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The exercise involved a wide range of naval operations, including anti-submarine warfare, air defense drills, and surface combat maneuvers.
• 'Varuna' aims to enhance interoperability, share best practices, and strengthen maritime cooperation between the two navies.
• This joint exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in ensuring security and stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• The collaboration is vital for addressing common maritime challenges, including piracy, counter-terrorism, and safeguarding sea lanes of communication.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Name of the joint naval exercise between India and France.
Location of the exercise (Indian Ocean Region).
Other major bilateral military exercises India conducts with key partners.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze how joint naval exercises like 'Varuna' contribute to strengthening India's defense capabilities and fostering international cooperation.
Evaluate the evolving strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in the context of the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the common maritime security challenges faced by nations in the IOR and the role of multilateral cooperation in addressing them.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships conducting joint maneuvers in the ocean.
GS2.trivia: India and France also conduct joint army exercise 'Shakti' and air force exercise 'Garuda'.

GS2.headline: Government Overhauls Public Grievance Redressal System for Enhanced Efficiency and Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) announced a major overhaul of the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS).
• The updated system incorporates AI-driven analytics, faster response mechanisms, and a citizen-centric feedback loop.
• The aim is to improve the speed and quality of grievance redressal, making government services more accessible and accountable.
• Training programs for government officials on effective grievance handling and citizen communication are also being rolled out.
• This initiative reflects the government's commitment to 'Good Governance' and leveraging technology for improved public service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy.
GS2.source: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG)
prelimsPoints: Full form of CPGRAMS and DARPG.
Key features of the overhauled system (AI, faster response).
Concept of 'Good Governance'.
Examples of other e-governance initiatives in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of an effective public grievance redressal mechanism in a democratic and welfare state.
Analyze how the integration of technology and AI can enhance the efficiency and transparency of public service delivery.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing administrative reforms and ensuring their sustained impact on citizen satisfaction.
Suggest measures to foster a culture of accountability and responsiveness within the bureaucracy.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand pressing a 'feedback' button on a digital interface.
GS2.trivia: CPGRAMS was launched in 2007 as an online system for citizens to lodge grievances with Central Government organizations.

GS2.headline: India Proposes Global Roadmap for Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Digital Economy Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India, hosting the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, proposed a global roadmap for leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for inclusive growth.
• The proposal emphasizes interoperable digital solutions like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as models for developing nations.
• Discussions focused on addressing the digital divide, promoting cross-border data flows with privacy safeguards, and fostering digital skill development.
• India highlighted its success in building DPI at scale, demonstrating its potential for financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
• The initiative aims to build a common framework for digital cooperation among G20 nations and extend benefits globally.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Key examples of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (Aadhaar, UPI).
Objectives of G20 Digital Economy Working Group.
Concept of 'digital divide'.
Significance of India's G20 leadership in digital transformation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its potential to drive inclusive growth and development, citing Indian examples.
Analyze India's role and proposals at the G20 Digital Economy Meeting and their implications for global digital cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in promoting cross-border data flows while ensuring data privacy and security.
Suggest how developing nations can leverage DPI models to accelerate their digital transformation and achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with digital network lines in the background.
GS2.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems, processing billions of transactions monthly.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Conducts High-Level Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, vocational education integration, teacher training, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, resource allocation, and achieving equitable access to quality education were discussed.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century, promoting holistic and multidisciplinary learning.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to accelerating implementation, with a focus on foundational literacy and numeracy, and early childhood care and education.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Year of launch of National Education Policy.
Key pillars of NEP 2020 (e.g., access, equity, quality, accountability).
Concept of 'foundational literacy and numeracy'.
Role of vocational education in NEP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Analyze the key reforms proposed under NEP 2020 and their expected impact on students, teachers, and institutions.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, funding, and teacher readiness.
Suggest strategies for ensuring equitable access to quality education and promoting holistic development across all levels of schooling.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reform.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those of 1968 and 1986.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Comprehensive Green Hydrogen Policy to Boost Energy Transition, Achieve Net Zero Goals
GS3.explainer:
• The policy aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• It outlines significant incentives for production, demand creation across key sectors, and robust R&D.
• Crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors such as refining, fertilizers, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
• Expected to create significant employment opportunities, attract substantial domestic and foreign investment, and reduce import dependency.
• This initiative is a cornerstone of India's commitment to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070 and enhance energy security.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure: Energy
Investment Models
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Conservation
Science & Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target for green hydrogen production capacity.
Key components of the policy: incentives, R&D, demand creation, manufacturing.
Applications of green hydrogen in various industrial sectors.
Year of India's Net Zero target (2070).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the economic benefits (job creation, export potential, investment) and challenges (cost, infrastructure, technology) of the policy.
Evaluate the environmental impact and contribution of green hydrogen to India's climate goals.
Examine the role of government incentives and private sector participation in achieving the policy objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and electrolysers.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Governor Expresses Cautious Optimism on Economic Outlook Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor, in a post-Monetary Policy Review statement, indicated cautious optimism regarding India's economic trajectory.
• While acknowledging global economic uncertainties and inflationary pressures, the Governor highlighted India's robust domestic demand and resilient financial sector.
• Key focus areas include maintaining price stability, supporting growth, and ensuring financial sector resilience against external shocks.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to data-driven policy decisions, with a flexible approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts suggest that while inflation remains a concern, the central bank sees room for sustained growth driven by government capital expenditure and private consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation and its impact
prelimsPoints: Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy.
Instruments of monetary policy (Repo rate, CRR, etc.).
Current inflation target range set by the government for RBI.
Meaning of 'cautious optimism' in economic context.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the impact of global economic headwinds (e.g., geopolitical conflicts, supply chain disruptions) on India's economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in achieving its objectives.
Suggest fiscal and structural reforms that could complement monetary policy efforts to ensure sustainable economic growth.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI headquarters building in Mumbai with the Indian Rupee symbol.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-X' Enhancing Disaster Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone with the successful launch of 'EOS-X', an advanced Earth Observation Satellite.
• Launched aboard the PSLV-CXX rocket from Sriharikota, EOS-X is designed to provide high-resolution imagery and data for disaster management, agriculture, and urban planning.
• The satellite features enhanced spectral bands and improved revisit capabilities, offering more timely and accurate information.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for societal benefit.
• Data from EOS-X will be crucial for predicting natural disasters, monitoring crop health, and assessing environmental changes.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Full form of ISRO and its primary objectives.
Differences between PSLV and GSLV.
Applications of Earth Observation Satellites.
Location of India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology, particularly Earth Observation Satellites, in disaster management and sustainable development in India.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous capabilities contribute to India's strategic autonomy and global standing in space.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits derived from satellite data in sectors like agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space program, including private sector participation and international collaborations.
GS3.imageDescription: ISRO's PSLV rocket launching into space from Sriharikota.
GS3.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites) in 2017.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Critical Threat to Western Ghats Biodiversity, Urges Enhanced Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) has flagged an alarming rate of biodiversity loss in the Western Ghats.
• The report points to increasing habitat fragmentation, climate change impacts, and unsustainable developmental activities as primary drivers of this decline.
• It calls for immediate and enhanced conservation measures, including stricter enforcement of environmental regulations and community-based conservation models.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• The study emphasizes the critical role of the Western Ghats in India's ecological balance, including its influence on monsoon patterns and water security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Climate change
prelimsPoints: Location and geographical extent of the Western Ghats.
Status of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Major threats identified in the report.
Examples of endemic species found in the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the recommendations of the NCBS report and suggest a multi-stakeholder approach for effective conservation.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing development needs with environmental protection in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
Examine the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in biodiversity conservation efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest and a waterfall.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 to Bolster Digital Defenses
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has launched India's updated National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure, promoting cyber resilience, and fostering a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It emphasizes a multi-pronged approach involving government, industry, academia, and international cooperation to combat evolving cyber threats.
• Key components include enhancing threat intelligence sharing, establishing specialized cyber incident response teams, and developing indigenous cybersecurity solutions.
• This updated strategy is crucial for protecting India's digital economy, national security, and citizens' data in an increasingly interconnected world.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Cyber Security
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Nodal agency for cybersecurity in India (CERT-In).
Key pillars of the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0.
Importance of Critical Information Infrastructure.
The Information Technology Act, 2000.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key features of the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 and its potential effectiveness in building cyber resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cybersecurity framework, including technological, human, and legal aspects.
Suggest measures for enhancing international cooperation in combating transboundary cybercrime and cyber warfare.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a map of India, representing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally, making robust cybersecurity strategies essential.

GS3.headline: Monsoon Progress Fuels Optimism for Kharif Crops, Government Assures MSP Support to Farmers
GS3.explainer:
• Favorable monsoon rainfall across key agricultural regions has generated optimism for a robust Kharif crop yield this year.
• The Ministry of Agriculture reported significant progress in sowing operations, particularly for rice, pulses, and oilseeds.
• The government reiterated its commitment to ensuring Minimum Support Price (MSP) for major crops, aiming to safeguard farmers' income.
• Timely monsoon has alleviated concerns over potential drought conditions in some areas earlier in the season.
• This positive agricultural outlook is crucial for rural economy stability, food security, and controlling food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: Major crops grown during Kharif season.
Purpose of Minimum Support Price (MSP).
Factors influencing monsoon rainfall in India.
Role of the Food Corporation of India (FCI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the monsoon in India's agricultural economy and its impact on food security.
Analyze the effectiveness of Minimum Support Price (MSP) in ensuring farmers' income and its implications for agricultural markets.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian agriculture, including climate change, market access, and infrastructure deficits.
Suggest policy measures to enhance agricultural productivity, farmer welfare, and resilience against climatic shocks.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a lush green paddy field during monsoon season.
GS3.trivia: Kharif crops are also known as 'monsoon crops' and are typically sown with the onset of the first rains in July.

GS3.headline: INS Vikrant, India's Indigenous Aircraft Carrier, Completes Maiden Operational Deployment
GS3.explainer:
• INS Vikrant, India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier, successfully concluded its maiden operational deployment in the Arabian Sea.
• The deployment involved extensive flight operations, integration of various fighter aircraft, and complex tactical maneuvers with other naval assets.
• This milestone significantly enhances India's maritime power projection capabilities and reinforces its position as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The successful deployment showcases India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing and advanced naval technology.
• INS Vikrant's operational readiness is crucial for safeguarding India's vast coastline, island territories, and strategic maritime interests.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Name of India's first indigenous aircraft carrier.
Significance of an aircraft carrier in naval power.
Operational area of the deployment (Arabian Sea).
Concept of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of aircraft carriers in modern naval warfare and India's maritime doctrine.
Analyze the significance of INS Vikrant's indigenous design and construction for India's defense self-reliance and technological capabilities.
Evaluate how the deployment of INS Vikrant enhances India's power projection and security role in the Indian Ocean Region.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's naval modernization program, including the acquisition of more indigenous platforms.
GS3.imageDescription: INS Vikrant sailing in the open sea with fighter jets on its deck.
GS3.trivia: INS Vikrant is named after India's first aircraft carrier, which was acquired from the United Kingdom in 1961.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG)
